format_metadata.title: Wexler Library Study Room Reservation Schedule

items.text:
Wexler Library — Study Room Reservation Schedule
Effective Monday, October 6

Students may reserve a group study room on the third floor of Wexler Library using the library portal. Please note the following rates and rules:

- Rooms 301-305 (seats 4): free for the first two hours, then $1.00 per hour.
- Rooms 310-312 (seats 8): $2.00 per hour, with a two-hour minimum.
- The media room (Room 320): $5.00 per hour; a projector and screen are included.

Reservation hours run from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM, seven days a week. Each student may hold no more than two bookings at one time. A reserved room is held for fifteen minutes after the start time; if no one arrives, the booking is released to other students.

To cancel, sign in to the portal at least one hour ahead to avoid a $3.00 no-show charge. Questions? Email the front desk at studyrooms@wexler.edu.

questions.stem: According to the schedule, what is the cost of using one of Rooms 301-305 for the first two hours?
questions.explanation: The schedule states that Rooms 301-305 are 'free for the first two hours,' so there is no charge during that period.

questions.stem: According to the schedule, what happens if a student does not arrive within fifteen minutes of the start time?

options.A: The room rate doubles for the booking
options.B: The student loses portal access for a day
options.C: The booking is released to other students
options.D: The reservation moves to a different room

questions.explanation: The schedule says a reserved room is held for fifteen minutes, and if no one arrives, 'the booking is released to other students.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about reserving the media room instead of Room 310?

options.A: It can be booked for longer periods
options.B: It comes with equipment the others lack
options.C: It is located on a different floor
options.D: It may be reserved without a portal account

questions.explanation: The media room listing notes 'a projector and screen are included,' which is not mentioned for the other rooms, implying it offers equipment the others do not.

format_metadata.title: The Birch Counter — Weekday Lunch Menu

items.text:
The Birch Counter — Weekday Lunch Menu
Served Monday to Friday, 11:00 AM to 2:30 PM, Student Union ground floor

Bowls
- Roasted Vegetable Bowl — $7.50
- Grilled Chicken Bowl — $8.50
- Tofu Noodle Bowl — $7.00 (vegan)

Sandwiches
- Turkey Club — $6.50
- Caprese Melt — $6.00

Sides and Drinks
- Soup of the day — $3.00
- Bottled juice or iced tea — $2.00
- Coffee — free refills with any bowl

Please note: every bowl comes with a side of bread at no extra cost. Students who bring their own reusable cup receive a $0.50 discount on any drink. We accept the campus meal card and cards, but the counter is cash-free. To skip the line, order ahead through the dining portal before 10:30 AM and pick up at the express window.

questions.stem: According to the menu, which item is the least expensive bowl?
questions.explanation: Among the bowls, the Tofu Noodle Bowl is priced at $7.00, lower than the Roasted Vegetable Bowl ($7.50) and the Grilled Chicken Bowl ($8.50). The Caprese Melt is a sandwich, not a bowl.

questions.stem: According to the menu, how can a student receive a discount on a drink?

options.A: By ordering a soup of the day
options.C: By paying with the meal card
options.D: By picking up at the express window

questions.explanation: The menu states that students who bring their own reusable cup receive a $0.50 discount on any drink.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about paying at The Birch Counter?

options.A: Tips are added automatically to each order
options.B: Coins and bills will not be accepted
options.C: Prices rise after the lunch rush ends
options.D: Drinks must be purchased before bowls

questions.explanation: The menu says the counter accepts the meal card and cards but is 'cash-free,' which implies that coins and bills will not be accepted.
